text.title: Chroma — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules: distance metric set once at creation, string IDs, get_or_create idempotent, single-process writes, explicit persistence path.

rule.statement: Distance metric MUST be set at collection creation via `metadata={"hnsw:space": "cosine"|"l2"|"ip"}`. Changing the metric requires recreating the collection and re-upserting all vectors.
rule.rationale: The metric is baked into the HNSW index structure; runtime change is not supported.

rule.statement: IDs MUST be strings. Integer IDs MUST be stringified before passing to Chroma APIs.
rule.rationale: Chroma enforces str-typed IDs; passing integers raises a TypeError.

rule.statement: Ingestion code MUST use `get_or_create_collection` (never `create_collection`), and `upsert` (never `add`), so re-runs are idempotent.
rule.rationale: `create_collection` raises on existing name; `add` errors on duplicate IDs. Idempotent variants make notebooks and CI re-runs safe.

rule.statement: Chroma PersistentClient MUST be accessed by a single writer process at a time. Concurrent writers WILL corrupt the SQLite store.
rule.rationale: SQLite locking is not safe for concurrent multi-process writes through Chroma's stack; corruption is silent and irrecoverable without backups.

rule.statement: Production-adjacent code MUST use `PersistentClient(path=...)`; in-memory `Client()` is restricted to ephemeral tests because all data vanishes on process exit.
rule.rationale: Developers conflate `Client()` with persisted state; explicit path forces an intentional persistence decision.
